content: Lowering the trigger threshold from 3.7 to 3.2 will make the algorithm more sensitive, potentially identifying sepsis cases earlier during flu season when inflammatory responses may be more common. However, this may also increase false positives and rapid response activations. Consider reviewing historical data from previous flu seasons to assess the trade-off between sensitivity and specificity at the 3.2 threshold before implementing the change. Additionally, ensure clinical teams are prepared for a possible increase in alerts.
